objective: Record final live Cloudflare Access Stage D proof evidence for Remote MCP Bridge epic #109 and add the operator controls needed for stdlib Python proof traffic through the Cloudflare edge.
scope_boundary: Modify only the governance Stage D proof runner, thin client user-agent support, Remote MCP runbook, issue #370 planning/validation/closeout artifacts, execution scope, and non-secret live audit evidence.
Use downstream local-ai-machine PRs for origin auth fixes; do not vendor downstream code into governance.
Do not commit Cloudflare credentials, service-token secrets, JWTs, private HMAC keys, or raw sensitive payloads.
out_of_scope: Changing unrelated governance standards or registries.
Changing unrelated Cloudflare applications, tunnels, or policies.
Keeping temporary Cloudflare service tokens after proof.
Closing epic #109 before final evidence and CI pass.
research_summary: Cloudflare Access live proof required observing actual edge behavior. Python's default user agent hit Cloudflare edge blocking, local resolver propagation required a curl-style resolve override, Access forwarded its own assertion before the inner bearer, and service-token traffic forwarded partial identity headers. Governance now documents proof-client controls while downstream local-ai-machine PRs #488, #490, and #492 handle origin auth semantics. The final proof passed through remote-mcp.hldpro.com with strict audit verification.

sprints.name: Live Cloudflare proof closeout
sprints.goal: Pass every Stage D AC against the Cloudflare-protected Remote MCP route and preserve non-secret evidence.
tasks: Add user-agent support to Stage D proof runner and thin client.
Add resolver override support to Stage D proof runner for DNS propagation parity with curl --resolve.
Document live proof environment controls in the service runbook.
Create issue #370 planning, execution-scope, validation, and closeout artifacts.
Run final Stage D proof through Cloudflare Access with a temporary service token.
Strictly verify live audit evidence and preserve JSONL plus manifest files.
Confirm temporary Cloudflare service token removal and deletion.
acceptance_criteria: Authenticated ping passes through Cloudflare Access plus inner token.
Anonymous request is rejected by Cloudflare or bridge.
Spoofed origin is rejected before dispatch and audited.
PII-bearing handoff is rejected before dispatch and audited without raw PII.
Audit verifier passes valid live evidence.
Local stdio MCP import proof passes after tunnel proof.
Temporary Cloudflare Access service token is removed from policy and deleted.
Evidence contains no raw PII, JWTs, service-token secrets, Cloudflare API tokens, or private HMAC keys.

specialist_reviews.focus: Confirm live evidence and validation notes are payload-safe and scoped to issue #370.
specialist_reviews.summary: The preserved audit artifacts contain audit metadata and HMAC digests only. Temporary Cloudflare service-token values, JWTs, private HMAC keys, and API tokens were not committed.

alternate_model_review.summary: This slice adds bounded proof-runner controls and records deterministic live evidence; downstream auth changes were separately tested and merged.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #370 may add proof-runner/client controls, runbook updates, live proof evidence, and governance closeout artifacts for epic #109.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Run governance validation, publish PR, merge if green, and close epic #109 after final evidence is on main.

material_deviation_rules: If any committed evidence contains a secret or raw sensitive payload, remove it, rotate affected credentials, and rerun proof.
If Cloudflare temporary token cleanup fails, do not merge until policy and token inventory are restored.
If live Stage D proof fails, do not close #109; record the issue-backed blocker instead.
